title: Meaning and scope of biotechnology
core: Biotechnology uses organisms, cells or enzymes to make useful products and processes. Traditional biotechnology used fermentation and breeding, while modern biotechnology works directly with DNA.
neetDepth: NEET questions often test the difference between traditional biotechnology and modern genetic engineering. Modern biotechnology is not just growing microbes; it includes recombinant DNA, PCR, gene transfer, diagnostics and transgenic organisms.
mustRemember: Organism/cell/enzyme can be the biological tool.
Useful product may be medicine, crop trait, diagnostic result or industrial molecule.
Modern biotechnology combines genetic engineering and bioprocess engineering.
diagramCue: Draw organism/cell/enzyme going through a controlled process to produce a useful product.
commonTrap: Do not define biotechnology only as genetic engineering. Genetic engineering is one important part of modern biotechnology.

title: Two core principles
core: The two central principles are genetic engineering and maintenance of sterile, contamination-free conditions for growth and product formation.
neetDepth: Genetic engineering creates new DNA combinations. Bioprocess engineering keeps the organism alive, pure, productive and scalable.
mustRemember: Sterile conditions prevent unwanted microbes from growing.
Bioprocessing includes scale-up and product recovery.
diagramCue: Show desired gene -> vector -> host -> expression -> product, with a sterile bioreactor alongside.
commonTrap: A correct recombinant DNA molecule is not enough; the product must still be produced and purified.

title: Isolation of desired DNA
core: Cells are broken open, proteins and RNA are removed, and DNA is precipitated using chilled ethanol so it can be collected.
neetDepth: Different cells need different wall-breaking enzymes: lysozyme for bacterial cell wall, cellulase for plant cell wall and chitinase for fungal cell wall. Protease removes proteins and RNase removes RNA.
mustRemember: DNA may appear as thread-like material.
Pure DNA is needed before restriction digestion or PCR.
diagramCue: Draw a tube with cell extract at bottom, ethanol layer above and white DNA threads between.
commonTrap: Do not say RNase removes proteins; protease removes proteins, RNase removes RNA.

title: Restriction enzymes
core: Restriction enzymes cut DNA at specific recognition sequences. Many recognition sequences are palindromic.
neetDepth: EcoRI recognises GAATTC and cuts between G and A to create sticky ends. Sticky ends can pair with complementary sticky ends and are sealed by ligase.
mustRemember: Sticky ends have overhangs; blunt ends do not.
diagramCue: Show 5'-GAATTC-3' and 3'-CTTAAG-5' with a cut between G and A.
commonTrap: Palindrome in DNA means same reading in 5' to 3' direction on both strands, not simply left-to-right on one strand.

title: Polymerase chain reaction
core: PCR amplifies a selected DNA region through repeated cycles of denaturation, annealing and extension.
neetDepth: Denaturation occurs around 95 C, annealing around 50-65 C and extension around 72 C. Taq polymerase is heat-stable and extends primers.
mustRemember: Taq polymerase is from Thermus aquaticus.
diagramCue: Draw double-stranded DNA separating, primers binding, and new strands extending.
commonTrap: PCR amplifies DNA; it does not directly amplify proteins.

title: Cloning vectors
core: A cloning vector carries foreign DNA into a host and replicates there. Plasmids are common vectors.
neetDepth: Important vector features include ori, selectable marker, cloning site and suitable copy number. A selectable marker helps identify transformed cells.
mustRemember: Cloning site accepts the foreign DNA insert.
diagramCue: Draw a circular plasmid with ori, marker, restriction site and inserted gene labelled.
commonTrap: Do not confuse vector with host. The vector carries DNA; the host cell maintains or expresses it.

title: Competent host and transformation
core: Host cells must be made competent to take up recombinant DNA. Bacterial transformation commonly uses CaCl2 and heat shock.
neetDepth: Other transfer methods include microinjection, gene gun and disarmed pathogen vectors. Agrobacterium-mediated transfer is important in plant biotechnology.
mustRemember: Competent means able to take up DNA.
Agrobacterium is a natural plant gene transfer system.
diagramCue: Draw plasmid outside a bacterial cell, heat shock pulse, and plasmid inside the cell.
commonTrap: Transformation and transduction are not the same word in all contexts; here transformation means uptake of foreign DNA by host cells.

title: Selection of recombinants
core: Selectable markers and screening methods help identify cells that contain recombinant DNA.
neetDepth: Antibiotic resistance markers distinguish transformed from non-transformed cells. Insertional inactivation can distinguish recombinant from non-recombinant transformants.
mustRemember: Blue-white screening is based on insertional inactivation concept.
diagramCue: Draw a petri plate with surviving colonies and separate blue/white colony outcomes.
commonTrap: A transformed cell may contain a non-recombinant vector. Selection and screening are not identical.

title: Gel electrophoresis
core: DNA fragments are separated in agarose gel under an electric field. DNA moves toward the positive electrode.
neetDepth: Smaller DNA fragments migrate farther because they pass through gel pores more easily. DNA bands can be cut from the gel and recovered by elution.
diagramCue: Draw wells near the negative side and bands moving toward the positive side.
commonTrap: Do not say larger fragments move faster. Larger fragments move slower and stay closer to wells.

title: Bioreactors
core: Bioreactors provide controlled large-scale conditions for growth and product formation.
neetDepth: A stirred-tank bioreactor includes agitator, sparger, pH control, temperature control, foam control, oxygen supply and sampling port.
diagramCue: Draw a stirred tank with impeller, sparger, inlet, outlet, pH and temperature labels.
commonTrap: A bioreactor is not just a storage tank; it actively controls growth conditions.

title: Downstream processing
core: After production, the product is separated, purified, formulated, quality tested and released.
neetDepth: Therapeutic products may need clinical trials and strict quality control. Downstream processing converts a culture product into a usable product.
mustRemember: Quality control checks safety and activity.
diagramCue: Draw a pipeline: culture broth -> filter -> purification -> QC -> vial/package.
commonTrap: Expression of a gene is not the final commercial product. Recovery and purification are essential.

title: Human insulin production
core: Recombinant DNA technology can produce human insulin using microbial systems.
neetDepth: Proinsulin has A, B and C peptide. Mature insulin forms after C-peptide removal and connection of A and B chains by disulphide bonds.
mustRemember: Animal insulin could cause immune reactions.
Mature insulin has A and B chains.
C-peptide is absent in mature insulin.
diagramCue: Draw proinsulin with A, C and B regions, then remove C peptide and join A/B chains.
commonTrap: Do not say C-peptide is part of mature insulin.

title: Bt cotton
core: Bt cotton contains genes from Bacillus thuringiensis that produce Cry proteins toxic to specific insect pests.
neetDepth: Bt toxin is produced as an inactive protoxin and becomes active in the alkaline gut of the insect, damaging midgut epithelial cells.
mustRemember: Activation happens in alkaline insect gut.
diagramCue: Draw cotton plant, larva gut, protoxin activation and damaged gut lining.
commonTrap: Do not say Bt toxin is activated in the human stomach or inside the plant.

title: Gene therapy
core: Gene therapy attempts to correct or compensate for a defective gene by introducing a functional gene.
neetDepth: ADA deficiency is the standard example. Ex vivo therapy removes cells, corrects them outside the body and returns them.
mustRemember: ADA deficiency is a key example.
diagramCue: Draw patient cells removed -> corrected with normal gene -> returned to patient.
commonTrap: Somatic gene therapy does not change the germline.

title: RNA interference
core: RNAi is gene silencing by targeting matching mRNA for degradation or blocking.
neetDepth: dsRNA is processed into small RNAs that guide silencing of complementary mRNA. NCERT links RNAi with nematode-resistant tobacco plants.
diagramCue: Draw dsRNA -> small RNA guide -> target mRNA cut -> protein not formed.
commonTrap: RNAi does not remove the DNA gene; it prevents expression of its message.

title: Molecular diagnosis
core: Molecular diagnosis detects disease markers early using PCR, ELISA and DNA probes.
neetDepth: PCR amplifies tiny nucleic acid amounts, ELISA detects antigen-antibody interaction and DNA probes bind complementary sequences.
mustRemember: PCR detects low nucleic acid amounts.
diagramCue: Draw PCR result panel, ELISA plate with colour change and DNA probe binding a target.
commonTrap: Probe and primer are different tools even though both can be short nucleic acid sequences.

title: Ethical issues and GEAC
core: Biotechnology requires biosafety review, ethical evaluation, patent awareness and fair use of bioresources.
neetDepth: GEAC evaluates genetically engineered organisms and products for validity and safety. Biopiracy involves unfair exploitation of bioresources or traditional knowledge.
mustRemember: Biopiracy is an ethical and legal concern.
diagramCue: Draw research -> biosafety data -> GEAC review -> monitored release, plus benefit-sharing card.
commonTrap: Ethics is not anti-biotechnology; it is responsible biotechnology.
